abstract.text: See full list of methods here:
Apphud is an all-in-one growth infrastructure for subscription apps, giving marketing and product teams the data and tools they need to make better decisions.

inlineContent.text: — Integrate in-app purchases and subscriptions with just two lines of code. No server-side code required. Cross-platform support for iOS, iPadOS, macOS, tvOS, watchOS, visionOS and Android out of the box.
— Track every key subscription metric in one place: MRR, subscriber retention (cohorts), churn rate, ARPU, trial conversions, proceeds, refunds, and more.

— Forward subscription events to your favorite third-party platforms with automatic currency conversion. Choose from 18 integrations including AppsFlyer, Adjust, Branch, Firebase, Amplitude, Mixpanel, OneSignal, Facebook, and TikTok. Custom server-to-server webhooks and APIs are also supported.
— Test different in-app purchases and paywalls. Run experiments to find the combination of prices and paywall parameters that maximizes ROI.

— Design fully customizable paywalls in Figma and render them natively in your app — no HTML or hand-written UI code required. You get the flexibility of a design tool with the performance and feel of a native paywall.
— Overcome post-iOS 14.5 IDFA limitations. Run paid campaigns on Facebook or TikTok and attribute installs in real time with near 100% accuracy.

— Win back lapsed subscribers, reduce churn, capture cancellation insights, and send push notifications using Rules. Choose between manual, scheduled and automated rules, build the screens with our visual web editor, and analyze user stats for every screen you ship.

content.text: The easiest way to integrate in-app subscriptions

inlineContent.text: Apphud provides ready-to-use infrastructure for every kind of in-app purchase — subscriptions, consumables and non-consumables. Three lines of code is all you need to get started:

code: // Get a placement by identifier, then access its paywall
// Purchase a product from the paywall

inlineContent.text: Apphud SDK can render fully designed paywall screens straight from the Apphud dashboard, with no additional UI work in your app. Fetch and present them in just a few lines of code:

code: // Preload paywall screens for faster presentation
// Fetch and present a paywall screen
// Present the ready-to-use paywall screen controller

inlineContent.text: Build beautiful paywalls without writing UI code
A/B test different layouts and copy
Update designs over the air, with no app release
Tailor paywall variations to different user segments

Apphud lets you ship app-level remote configuration and A/B-test variations from the dashboard. The current user exposes the assigned experiment, variation and config payload, so you can branch your UI or business logic without shipping a new build.

code: guard let user = Apphud.currentUser() else { return }
// App-level remote config, parsed as a JSON dictionary
if let theme = config["onboarding_theme"] as? String {
// Or read the raw JSON string directly
// Active A/B experiment and variation, if any
// Number of devices associated with the same userId — handy for spotting account sharing
// limit premium features, prompt re-authentication, etc.

abstract.text: Implements the
mechanism. This method sends the current App Store Receipt to Apphud and returns information about the user’s subscriptions and in-app purchases.
Checks the eligibility for introductory offers for multiple products simultaneously.
Retrieves an
object based on its product identifier. This method is used to access product details for items you’ve added in the Apphud Dashboard under Product hub.
Removes the preloaded paywall Screen from memory cache.
Submits the device’s push token to Apphud. This is essential for enabling Apphud to send push notifications to the device.
Logs a “Paywall Shown” event which is required for A/B Testing analytics.
Represents the result of attempting to fetch and prepare a paywall screen for display.
Entry point of the Apphud SDK. It provides access to all its features.
Initializes Apphud SDK. Call this during the app’s launch.
Purchases a subscription with a promotional offer and automatically submits the App Store Receipt to Apphud.

objects that were added in the Apphud dashboard (Apphud > Product hub). This property returns

Handles an incoming push notification payload. This method is used to process Rules-related notifications created by Apphud.
Presents an Apphud Screen that was previously delayed for presentation. This is typically used in conjunction with the
delegate method, where returning
would delay the Screen’s presentation.

An enumeration for commonly used paywall identifiers in Apphud. Ensure that the identifiers used here match those in the Apphud Mission control -> Paywalls section. This enum facilitates the retrieval of specific paywall configurations in your code.
Public Callback object provide -> [String: Bool]
Retrieves all auto-renewable subscriptions that the user has ever purchased. This method is useful if your app has more than one subscription group.
Fetches
objects asynchronously from the App Store. This method is used to retrieve the products that you have configured in the Apphud dashboard (Apphud > Product hub).
Initializes Apphud SDK with User ID & Device ID pair. Not recommended for use unless you know what you are doing.
Sets a custom user property in Apphud. The value assigned to this property must be of one of the following types:
, or
Returns the current userID that identifies the user across multiple devices.
Restores the user’s purchases asynchronously. This method should be called when the user taps the “Restore” button in your app.
Grants a free promotional subscription to the user. This method updates the user’s subscription status, and after this, the
method will return
Returns an instance of the current user.
Called when user tapped on purchase button in Apphud purchase screen.
Called when there is a change in the user’s non-renewing purchases. This can occur when a new purchase is made or if a purchase is refunded.
Called when the Apphud SDK detects a purchase made outside of its standard purchase methods. This is particularly useful for handling purchases made with Promo Codes.
Checks whether the given product is eligible for any of its promotional offers.
Implements the mechanism for handling In-App Purchases initiated directly from the App Store page.
Updates the user ID. Use this when you need to change the user identifier during the app’s runtime.

Determines whether the user currently has premium access, either through an active auto-renewable subscription, non-renewing subscription or a non-consumable (lifetime) purchase.
Custom Apphud class containing all information about customer subscription.

An enumeration for commonly used placement identifiers in Apphud. Ensure that the identifiers used here match those in the Apphud Mission control -> Targetings section. This enum facilitates the retrieval of specific placement configurations in your code.
Notifies Apphud when a purchase process is initiated from a paywall in
, enabling the use of A/B experiments. This method should be called right before executing your own purchase method, and it’s specifically required only when the SDK is in Observer Mode.
This is Apphud’s internal class.

for a new user.

Retrieves the Apphud Rule associated with a Screen that was delayed for presentation.

Submits Device Identifiers (IDFA and IDFV) to Apphud. These identifiers may be required for marketing and attribution platforms such as AppsFlyer, Facebook, Singular, etc.

A placement is a specific location within a user’s journey (such as onboarding, settings, etc.) where its internal paywall is intended to be displayed.

Asynchronously fetches a paywall Screen for the given paywall.
Pass your own modal presentation style to Apphud Screens. This is useful since iOS 13 presents in page sheet style by default.
Called when the Apphud SDK detects a deferred or interrupted purchase. This can happen in scenarios like required Strong Customer Authentication (SCA), parental control approvals, etc.

A list of paywall placements, potentially altered based on the user’s involvement in A/B testing, if any. A placement is a specific location within a user’s journey (such as onboarding, settings, etc.) where its internal paywall is intended to be displayed.
Refreshes current user data including app remote config, paywalls, placements, subscriptions, non-renewing purchases, and promotionals.

Retrieves the most recently purchased auto-renewable subscription object for the current user. Subscriptions are cached on the device.

Returns the current device ID. Use this method if you need to implement a custom logout/login flow by saving a User ID & Device ID pair for each app user. This allows for a more controlled management of user sessions and device associations.
